title: Permissionless third-party locker creation enables locker squatting for arbitrary addresses
reason: `createLockerContract(address user)` appears intentionally permissionless for relayed/prepaid creation; locker ownership is still bound to `user`, so attacker control is not gained and concrete protocol-level harm is too speculative.

title: Batch unit-update signature hashing uses packed dynamic arrays without explicit length separation
reason: For `uint256[]` arrays, `abi.encodePacked` uses fixed 32-byte elements; with validated equal lengths, the encoded boundaries are deterministic for the provided calldata and no practical collision/reinterpretation exploit is supported.

title: SupVesting emergencyWithdraw allows admin to permanently delete any recipient's vesting
reason: This is an explicit privileged emergency mechanism (`onlyAdmin`) rather than an unintended permission bypass; it is a trust-model choice, not a standalone code vulnerability.

title: StakingRewardController distributeTaxAdjustment is permissionless enabling front-running
FluidEPProgramManager startFunding lacks active program check allowing duplicate funding
EPProgramManager batchUpdateUserUnits doesn't verify programs share same token
reason: Batch updates only set per-program pool units and do not require a same-token invariant; no concrete cross-token exploit path is demonstrated from this omission alone.

title: FluidLockerFactory uses CREATE2 with user salt creating irreversible address commitment
reason: Premise is incorrect: lockers are BeaconProxy instances and existing lockers follow beacon implementation upgrades.

title: SupVestingFactory missing endDate validation allowing invalid vesting schedules
reason: Claim is incomplete and unsupported; invalid `endDate` inputs revert via checked arithmetic/division, so they do not create persistent malformed schedules.
